meta.question: What is the difference between the APS Black Box Specification at the JPL level and at the TMT level?
meta.finalAnswer:
The APS Black Box Specification exists at two levels with key differences:

**TMT Level (APS Black Box Specification TMT):**
- Higher-level, abstract specification
- Contains only 1 element: the `pplc` part property
- Located in: TMT::Project::Work Packages::Project Systems Engineering::01 Components::Operational::TMT Observatory System::Black Box Specifications for Suppliers
- Serves as the parent/general specification

**JPL Level (APS Operational Blackbox Specification JPL):**
- More detailed, operational specification
- Contains 30 elements including:
  - **12+ ports**: GCDataOut, PEAS2TCSIn, PEAS2TCSOut, PEAS2M1CSOut, GC2ESWIn, PEAS2ESWIn, PEAS2M1CSIn, GC2ESWOut, PEAS2CSOut, PEAS2CSIn, PEAS2ESWOut, APS2UserIn
  - **Timing properties**: ppls, on-axis alignment maximum time for Post Segment Exchange JPL, maintenance alignment maximum time, off-Axis Acquisition Maximum Time, m3 Alignment Maximum Time, warping Harness Maximum Time, self Test Maximum Time, telescope Pupil Alignment Requirement
- Located in: TMT::Project::Work Packages::Telescope::WorkPackages::Control::Alignment and Phasing System::Systems Engineering::01 Components::Operational::APS::Black Box Specification::Structure

**Relationship:** The JPL specification is a specialization (subclass) of the TMT specification via generalization, meaning JPL inherits from TMT but adds detailed interface ports and specific timing requirements for operational implementation.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?elem ?type ?name WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?name . 
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?name)), LCASE("APS Black Box"))) 
  ?elem rdf:type ?type .
}
LIMIT 25

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?gen ?specific ?general WHERE {
  ?gen rdf:type vocab:generalization .
  ?gen vocab:Generalization_specific ?specific .
  ?gen vocab:Generalization_general ?general .
  FILTER(STR(?specific) = "http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/TMT/block/TMT::Project::Work%20Packages::Telescope::WorkPackages::Control::Alignment%20and%20Phasing%20System::Systems%20Engineering::01%20Components::Operational::APS::Black%20Box%20Specification::Structure::APS%20Operational%20Blackbox%20Specification%20JPL" ||
         STR(?specific) = "http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/TMT/block/TMT::Project::Work%20Packages::Project%20Systems%20Engineering::01%20Components::Operational::TMT%20Observatory%20System::Black%20Box%20Specifications%20for%20Suppliers::APS%20Black%20Box%20Specification%20TMT")
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: Found the key relationship: JPL spec (APS Operational Blackbox Specification JPL) is a specialization of TMT spec (APS Black Box Specification TMT) via generalization. The JPL spec has 30 elements including 12+ ports (GCDataOut, PEAS2TCSIn, PEAS2TCSOut, PEAS2M1CSOut, GC2ESWIn, PEAS2ESWIn, PEAS2M1CSIn, GC2ESWOut, PEAS2CSOut, PEAS2CSIn, PEAS2ESWOut, APS2UserIn) and multiple timing properties (ppls, on-axis alignment maximum time, maintenance alignment maximum time, etc.). The TMT spec has only 1 element (pplc) and serves as the parent/supplier specification. This shows JPL level is more detailed with specific interface ports and timing requirements, while TMT level is a higher-level abstract specification.

request.answer:
The APS Black Box Specification exists at two levels with key differences:

**TMT Level (APS Black Box Specification TMT):**
- Higher-level, abstract specification
- Contains only 1 element: the `pplc` part property
- Located in: TMT::Project::Work Packages::Project Systems Engineering::01 Components::Operational::TMT Observatory System::Black Box Specifications for Suppliers
- Serves as the parent/general specification

**JPL Level (APS Operational Blackbox Specification JPL):**
- More detailed, operational specification
- Contains 30 elements including:
  - **12+ ports**: GCDataOut, PEAS2TCSIn, PEAS2TCSOut, PEAS2M1CSOut, GC2ESWIn, PEAS2ESWIn, PEAS2M1CSIn, GC2ESWOut, PEAS2CSOut, PEAS2CSIn, PEAS2ESWOut, APS2UserIn
  - **Timing properties**: ppls, on-axis alignment maximum time for Post Segment Exchange JPL, maintenance alignment maximum time, off-Axis Acquisition Maximum Time, m3 Alignment Maximum Time, warping Harness Maximum Time, self Test Maximum Time, telescope Pupil Alignment Requirement
- Located in: TMT::Project::Work Packages::Telescope::WorkPackages::Control::Alignment and Phasing System::Systems Engineering::01 Components::Operational::APS::Black Box Specification::Structure

**Relationship:** The JPL specification is a specialization (subclass) of the TMT specification via generalization, meaning JPL inherits from TMT but adds detailed interface ports and specific timing requirements for operational implementation.
